creator: @soyenriquerocha
executive_summary: The aggressive Tododeia portfolio for March 2026 is built around three conviction pillars: (1) the AI supercycle, expressed through concentrated positions in AVGO and NVDA as the infrastructure layer, PLTR as the software application layer, and copper as the physical build-out layer; (2) a commodity inflation and safe-haven complex anchored in gold and silver, which benefit from the Iran war shock and structural supply deficits without the reversal risk of crude oil; and (3) a high-conviction crypto regulatory recovery trade in XRP following the landmark SEC/CFTC derisking event. The portfolio deliberately avoids direct crude oil exposure despite the 61% YTD run, preferring to capture the geopolitical premium through gold and silver while the oil trade approaches exhaustion. A tactical USD/JPY short near the 160 intervention trigger provides both currency alpha and a portfolio hedge against eventual geopolitical de-escalation and USD safe-haven premium unwinding, while a 7% cash reserve maintains optionality for Iran war tail-risk deployment.

macro_environment.summary: The March 2026 macro backdrop is defined by three dominant forces: (1) the Iran war triggering the worst energy supply disruption in history, driving oil +61% YTD and gold to near-ATH levels; (2) the AI supercycle accelerating at breakneck pace, with NVDA/AVGO printing blowout earnings and AI tokens surging on Nvidia GTC catalysts; (3) a hawkish Fed holding at 3.5-3.75% with only one cut left in 2026, while inflation remains sticky on oil-driven PPI. Crypto markets are in a fear cycle (Fear & Greed 29) but regulatory tailwinds — XRP/SOL commodity classification — represent a structural derisking event. The DXY reclaiming 100 creates a ceiling for risk assets, but concentrated AI and commodity plays offer compelling asymmetric upside.

warnings: Iran war tail risk: An escalation to Strait of Hormuz closure would cause severe dislocation across ALL asset classes simultaneously — oil to $150+, gold to $6,000+, but equities and crypto would face violent risk-off selling. Crypto fear cycle (Fear & Greed 29): The aggregate crypto market remains in fear territory. Individual regulatory wins (XRP) can outperform the sector, but a broader market capitulation event could drag all crypto assets lower regardless of fundamentals.
Fed hawkish hold duration risk: If inflation re-accelerates on oil shock, the Fed could signal further hikes — significant headwind for high-multiple AI stocks (NVDA, PLTR) and crypto simultaneously.

USD/JPY short timing risk: Japanese intervention is unpredictable in exact timing. Use options (USD/JPY put) rather than spot short to limit maximum loss to premium paid.

Sterling viewed as relatively resilient EM-adjacent currency given BoE's reluctance to cut amid inflation pressures
assets.reasoning: GBP benefited from a hawkish BoE surprise on March 19 and the pair recovered toward 1.34, but the UK's unique stagflation challenge — stubborn inflation meeting a weakening labor market with limited fiscal room — limits sustainable upside. The pair is likely to trade in a 1.32–1.36 range pending inflation data.

currencies.sector_summary: Global forex markets in the week of March 20, 2026 are dominated by a single macro driver: the ongoing Iran war and its energy shock, with Brent crude near $117/barrel reshaping inflation expectations and central bank outlooks worldwide. The week of March 16–19 was an unprecedented 'central bank super-week,' with the Fed, ECB, BoE, BoJ, BoC, SNB, and Riksbank all deciding policy in a three-day window — and all either held or signaled caution, underscoring the fog of war premium now embedded in rates markets. The US Dollar has reclaimed the 100 level on safe-haven demand but faces a ceiling as the easing cycle is delayed, not cancelled.
currencies.top_pick_reasoning: USD/JPY is trading near its 52-week high of 159.91 and approaching the 160 level that Japanese authorities have explicitly flagged as an intervention trigger. With the BoJ on a slow normalization path, explicit FX intervention warnings on the table, and the pair near multi-year highs in a geopolitically charged environment, the risk/reward for a short USD/JPY position (or long JPY trade) is the most asymmetric opportunity in the forex market right now. A return to the 150 area is a credible medium-term target, representing a 5%+ move.

Near-term pullback from ATH $5,626 provides an attractive re-entry with UBS targeting $6,200 by mid-year.

EIA forecasts Brent above $95/bbl for next two months before falling to ~$70 by year-end as conflict is expected to ease
WTI surged from $60.53 to near $100 in just 7 weeks — entirely geopolitics-driven; underlying supply fundamentals remain bearish

Energy analysts warn oil is a 'geopolitical trade' not a fundamental one — structurally bearish with OPEC+ and US shale supply glut intact

EIA expects prices to fall below $80 by Q3 2026 once Hormuz flows normalize, making new long positions risky at current levels.

Silver surged 148% in 2025 driven by fifth consecutive year of structural supply deficit (~63M oz) and record solar panel demand consuming 25%+ of annual supply
Citi set a $110/oz target for H2 2026 citing acute shortage of physical silver available for industrial delivery; BNP Paribas targets $100 by late 2026
AI data center servers consume 2-3x more silver than traditional equipment; data center power demand expected to double by 2026

The multi-year supply-demand imbalance makes dips to $65-70 historically attractive accumulation zones.

Institutional rotation into copper accelerating; major asset managers citing copper as top commodity pick for 2026 on supply-demand fundamentals

J.P. Morgan targets $12,500/tonne ($5.67/lb) for Q2 with Citi seeing $13,000–$15,000/tonne in a bull scenario.

assets.name: Natural Gas (Henry Hub)

Henry Hub averaged $7.72/MMBtu in January 2026 (81% above December) on Winter Storm Fern cold snap, then collapsed to $3.62 in February on mild weather and ample storage

assets.reasoning: Natural gas benefits near-term from the Qatar LNG hub attack with European benchmark prices surging 16.7%, but the underlying US Henry Hub market faces structural headwinds from record production, elevated storage, and seasonal injection season beginning in April. The Qatar disruption risk is a genuine wildcard but US nat-gas storage cushion limits sustained upside at Henry Hub.

materials.sector_summary: The commodities complex in March 2026 is bifurcated: precious metals (gold, silver) and energy (WTI, nat-gas) are being driven by the worst Middle East supply disruption in history — Iranian strikes on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG hub and the Strait of Hormuz closure have sent oil up 61% YTD and gold to near-record levels — while industrial metals like copper trade on structural supply-demand fundamentals independent of geopolitics. The Fed's rate hold on March 19 and a hotter-than-expected PPI print created a sharp near-term headwind for precious metals, pulling gold 7% and silver 12% off their recent peaks, but the structural bull case for monetary metals and electrification metals remains intact.
materials.top_pick_reasoning: Silver offers the best risk-reward in the commodities complex right now: it has pulled back sharply (-43% from its $121.79 ATH to ~$69.50) creating an attractive entry point, while structural fundamentals have never been stronger — five consecutive years of supply deficits, record solar panel and EV demand consuming 25%+ of annual supply, AI data centers driving incremental demand, and safe-haven flows from Middle East conflict. With Citi targeting $110 and BNP Paribas targeting $100 by late 2026, silver's current dip offers exceptional upside relative to gold, copper, and oil at current valuations.
